skill.name: agentdb-memory-patterns
skill.description: Implement persistent memory patterns for AI agents using AgentDB, including session memory, long-term storage, pattern learning, and context retrieval.
skill.summary: Build stateful AI agents with AgentDB memory, retrieval, learning, and ReasoningBank integration patterns.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, database, network, weak crypto, and dangerous-combination patterns. Review found the commands and database paths are documentation examples, but the skill does instruct users to run unpinned npx packages and add an MCP server, which creates medium supply-chain and tool-execution risk. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or confirmed weak cryptography was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned External Tool Execution in Setup Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run npx agentdb@latest commands and register an MCP server through Claude Code. This is not hidden execution, but it can download and run package code from the network and should be pinned or verified before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are directly present and use npx with the @latest tag. The risk is supply-chain and user-mediated execution, not automatic malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation Links Are Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are GitHub and project website references in the Learn More section. They do not send local data, credentials, or database contents.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as documentation links. No code path or command transmits data to those endpoints.

low_findings.title: SQLite Database Paths Are Local Usage Examples
low_findings.description: The .db references are local AgentDB and ReasoningBank paths used in examples for initialization, querying, migration, and statistics. They are not embedded secrets or credential stores.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The database paths are shown as local CLI and API examples. I found no evidence that the skill reads or exfiltrates existing sensitive databases.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Dangerous Combination Heuristics Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and dangerous-combination findings appear to be heuristic false positives. The reviewed content contains memory and database examples, but no confirmed weak hash algorithm, credential access, obfuscation, or prompt injection attempt.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines did not show MD5, SHA1, credential access, or obfuscated behavior. The critical heuristic is explained by documentation commands plus documentation URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Fetched CLI Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple examples use npx agentdb@latest, which fetches and runs a package version selected at execution time. This should be treated as user-approved external command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present in several bash examples. It is legitimate for setup, but unpinned network-fetched execution remains a meaningful risk.

content.value_statement: AI agents often lose useful context between sessions. This skill provides AgentDB patterns for persistent memory, retrieval, learning, and ReasoningBank migration.
actual_capabilities: Shows AgentDB CLI setup commands for local, custom-dimension, preset, and in-memory databases.
Explains how to start the AgentDB MCP server and add it to Claude Code.
Provides TypeScript patterns for session memory, long-term facts, pattern learning, and context retrieval.
Documents ReasoningBank migration and AgentDB adapter usage for learning and reasoning workflows.
Lists plugin templates, reasoning modules, performance guidance, and troubleshooting commands.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 and an AgentDB installation or package access.
Uses documentation examples rather than a complete application scaffold.
External commands use npx agentdb@latest, so users should verify package provenance.
Does not define privacy policy rules for storing personal conversation data.

use_cases.title: Add memory to a chat assistant
use_cases.description: Use session and long-term memory patterns to preserve user preferences, recent messages, and useful facts.

use_cases.title: Migrate ReasoningBank data
use_cases.description: Follow the migration pattern to move legacy ReasoningBank storage into an AgentDB-backed adapter.

use_cases.title: Prototype learning agents
use_cases.description: Use plugin templates and reasoning modules to test pattern learning, retrieval, and memory optimization.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic memory plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentdb-memory-patterns to design a simple session memory layer for my chat assistant.

prompt_templates.title: Choose storage patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentdb-memory-patterns to compare session memory, long-term facts, and pattern learning for my agent.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a ReasoningBank migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentdb-memory-patterns to outline a safe migration from ReasoningBank to AgentDB for my project.

prompt_templates.title: Design an optimized memory architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentdb-memory-patterns to design hierarchical memory, consolidation, retrieval, and learning plugin usage for a production agent.

output_examples.input: Design memory for a support assistant that remembers users across sessions.
output_examples.output: A structured plan with session history, long-term preference facts, retrieval settings, and privacy checks for stored conversation data.

output_examples.input: Help me migrate an existing ReasoningBank database to AgentDB.
output_examples.output: A migration checklist covering source and destination paths, validation steps, adapter setup, and post-migration retrieval testing.

output_examples.input: Improve search speed for a growing agent memory store.
output_examples.output: Recommendations for HNSW indexing, cache sizing, quantization choices, batch operations, and monitoring with database statistics.

best_practices: Pin package versions before running setup commands in production environments.
Store only necessary user data and define retention rules before enabling long-term memory.
Test retrieval quality with realistic prompts before enabling automated reasoning or learning.
anti_patterns: Do not store secrets, credentials, or private user data without explicit controls.
Do not run unverified latest-version CLI packages inside sensitive environments.
Do not treat memory retrieval results as authoritative without validation.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build AI agents that store, retrieve, and organize memory with AgentDB.

faq.question: Does it include executable source files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a markdown guide with CLI and TypeScript examples.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents an AgentDB MCP server setup for Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the listed commands?
faq.answer: Review and pin package versions first. Several examples use npx with the latest package tag.

faq.question: Does it cover ReasoningBank migration?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a migration example and adapter usage for ReasoningBank integration.

faq.question: Does it manage privacy automatically?
faq.answer: No. You must define retention, consent, and filtering policies for stored memory.
